title: Debugger Browser
text: Debugger Browser Use the Debugger Browser to view the structure and ProcScript modules of components and Global ProcScript library. You can also toggle breakpoints in a ProcScript module, watch fields and variables, or change their values. The type of object determines what is displayed. By default, the current component is open. You can open another component or global ProcScript for examination. Fields The following fields are available: Structure —structure of the current component or Global ProcScript, showing entities and fields, nonempty triggers, local ProcScript modules, and a list of component or global variables. Trigger / Module — contents of the ProcScript module for the item selected in the Structure field. Structure Displays the tree structure of the currently open component or global ProcScript. Table 1. Tree items for Debugger structure types Structure type Tree items Component The entities and fields, all nonempty triggers, local ProcScript modules, and a list of component variables. Library The global ProcScript module currently open and a list of all global variables in the library. Trigger / Module Displays the contents of the ProcScript module for the item selected in the Structure field. Debugger Browser information For more information about the Debugger Browser, refer to: Browsing ProcScript Modules in the Uniface Debugger . Related Links Working with watches Add watches from the debugger main window or from the browser. Updating fields, variables, or watch values Update watch fields, variables, or values.
